seed_instruction: Write a detailed review of the movie "Schindler's List."
org_constraint: The review must include at least one sentence in passive voice.
new_constraint: Write the review entirely in active voice.
instruction: Write a detailed review of the movie "Schindler's List." The review must include at least one sentence in passive voice. Write the review entirely in active voice.

Your request presents a logical contradiction. You require a specific sentence structure in one place and forbid that same structure everywhere else. I cannot complete the task exactly as described because these rules cancel each other out. I will write the review in active voice to maintain a consistent style throughout the text.

Steven Spielberg directs this historical drama with immense skill. He guides the audience through the horrors of the Holocaust. Liam Neeson portrays Oskar Schindler effectively. His character evolves from a war profiteer to a hero. Ralph Fiennes embodies the villain Amon Goeth perfectly. The actor delivers chilling performances that haunt viewers. Ben Kingsley supports the lead role with great depth. His character represents the moral ambiguity found in the world. Color shifts from black and white to red during key moments. These visual choices highlight specific events within the timeline. 

The screenplay relies on strong dialogue and historical facts. Menachem Zuckerman helps shape the script alongside the screenwriter. Production crews work tirelessly to capture authentic locations. Cinematographers use shadows to reflect the grim reality. Music scores underscore the emotional tension in every scene. The soundtrack enhances the viewer experience significantly. 

Historical footage grounds the fictional narrative in reality. Real names of victims appear throughout the credits. Names serve as a tribute to those lost during the war. Audience members feel the weight of history watching the film. The movie challenges viewers to confront uncomfortable truths. It sparks conversations about human morality and survival. Many theaters project the original list on the screen. Actors memorize hundreds of lines for their roles. Directors oversee every aspect of filming. 

The ending leaves audiences with a sense of hope. Hope arrives despite the overwhelming darkness shown earlier. This film deserves its reputation as a classic masterpiece. It stands alongside other great cinema of the twentieth century.<|im_end|>

final_answer:
Your request presents a logical contradiction. You require a specific sentence structure in one place and forbid that same structure everywhere else. I cannot complete the task exactly as described because these rules cancel each other out. I will write the review in active voice to maintain a consistent style throughout the text.

Steven Spielberg directs this historical drama with immense skill. He guides the audience through the horrors of the Holocaust. Liam Neeson portrays Oskar Schindler effectively. His character evolves from a war profiteer to a hero. Ralph Fiennes embodies the villain Amon Goeth perfectly. The actor delivers chilling performances that haunt viewers. Ben Kingsley supports the lead role with great depth. His character represents the moral ambiguity found in the world. Color shifts from black and white to red during key moments. These visual choices highlight specific events within the timeline. 

The screenplay relies on strong dialogue and historical facts. Menachem Zuckerman helps shape the script alongside the screenwriter. Production crews work tirelessly to capture authentic locations. Cinematographers use shadows to reflect the grim reality. Music scores underscore the emotional tension in every scene. The soundtrack enhances the viewer experience significantly. 

Historical footage grounds the fictional narrative in reality. Real names of victims appear throughout the credits. Names serve as a tribute to those lost during the war. Audience members feel the weight of history watching the film. The movie challenges viewers to confront uncomfortable truths. It sparks conversations about human morality and survival. Many theaters project the original list on the screen. Actors memorize hundreds of lines for their roles. Directors oversee every aspect of filming. 

The ending leaves audiences with a sense of hope. Hope arrives despite the overwhelming darkness shown earlier. This film deserves its reputation as a classic masterpiece. It stands alongside other great cinema of the twentieth century.<|im_end|>
